verses.latin_vulgate: Viri fratres, et patres, audite quam ad vos nunc reddo rationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄνδρες ἀδελφοὶ καὶ πατέρες ἀκούσατέ μου τῆς πρὸς ὑμᾶς νῦν ἀπολογίας
verses.king_james: Men, brethren, and fathers, hear ye my defence *which I make* now unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum audissent autem quia hebræa lingua loqueretur ad illos, magis præstiterunt silentium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσαντες δὲ ὅτι τῇ ἑβραΐδι διαλέκτῳ προσεφώνει αὐτοῖς μᾶλλον παρέσχον ἡσυχίαν καὶ φησίν
verses.king_james: (And when they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicit: Ego sum vir Judæus, natus in Tarso Ciliciæ, nutritus autem in ista civitate, secus pedes Gamaliel eruditus juxta veritatem paternæ legis, æmulator legis, sicut et vos omnes estis hodie:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγώ μὲν εἰμι ἀνὴρ ἰουδαῖος γεγεννημένος ἐν ταρσῷ τῆς κιλικίας ἀνατεθραμμένος δὲ ἐν τῇ πόλει ταύτῃ παρὰ τοὺς πόδας γαμαλιὴλ πεπαιδευμένος κατὰ ἀκρίβειαν τοῦ πατρῴου νόμου ζηλωτὴς ὑπάρχων τοῦ θεοῦ καθὼς πάντες ὑμεῖς ἐστε σήμερον
verses.king_james: I am verily a man *which am* a Jew, born in Tarsus, *a city* in Cilicia, yet brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, *and* taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous toward God, as ye all are this day.

verses.latin_vulgate: qui hanc viam persecutus sum usque ad mortem, alligans et tradens in custodias viros ac mulieres,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς ταύτην τὴν ὁδὸν ἐδίωξα ἄχρι θανάτου δεσμεύων καὶ παραδιδοὺς εἰς φυλακὰς ἄνδρας τε καὶ γυναῖκας
verses.king_james: And I persecuted this way unto the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut princeps sacerdotum mihi testimonium reddit, et omnes majores natu: a quibus et epistolas accipiens, ad fratres Damascum pergebam, ut adducerem inde vinctos in Jerusalem ut punirentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς καὶ ὁ ἀρχιερεὺς μαρτυρεῖ μοι καὶ πᾶν τὸ πρεσβυτέριον παρ᾽ ὧν καὶ ἐπιστολὰς δεξάμενος πρὸς τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς εἰς δαμασκὸν ἐπορευόμην ἄξων καὶ τοὺς ἐκεῖσε ὄντας δεδεμένους εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ ἵνα τιμωρηθῶσιν
verses.king_james: As also the high priest doth bear me witness, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I received letters unto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there bound unto Jerusalem, for to be punished.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem, eunte me, et appropinquante Damasco media die, subito de cælo circumfulsit me lux copiosa:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δέ μοι πορευομένῳ καὶ ἐγγίζοντι τῇ δαμασκῷ περὶ μεσημβρίαν ἐξαίφνης ἐκ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ περιαστράψαι φῶς ἱκανὸν περὶ ἐμέ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that, as I made my journey, and was come nigh unto Damascus about noon, suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me.

verses.latin_vulgate: et decidens in terram, audivi vocem dicentem mihi: Saule, Saule, quid me persequeris?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔπεσον τε εἰς τὸ ἔδαφος καὶ ἤκουσα φωνῆς λεγούσης μοι σαοὺλ σαούλ τί με διώκεις
verses.king_james: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem respondi: Quis es, domine? Dixitque ad me: Ego sum Jesus Nazarenus, quem tu persequeris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ δὲ ἀπεκρίθην τίς εἶ κύριε εἶπέν τε πρός με ἐγώ εἰμι ἰησοῦς ὁ ναζωραῖος ὃν σὺ διώκεις
verses.king_james: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui mecum erant, lumen quidem viderunt, vocem autem non audierunt ejus qui loquebatur mecum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ σὺν ἐμοὶ ὄντες τὸ μὲν φῶς ἐθεάσαντο καὶ ἔμφοβοι ἐγένοντο τὴν δὲ φωνὴν οὐκ ἤκουσαν τοῦ λαλοῦντός μοι
verses.king_james: And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixi: Quid faciam, domine? Dominus autem dixit ad me: Surgens vade Damascum: et ibi tibi dicetur de omnibus quæ te oporteat facere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον δέ τί ποιήσω κύριε ὁ δὲ κύριος εἶπεν πρός με ἀναστὰς πορεύου εἰς δαμασκόν κἀκεῖ σοι λαληθήσεται περὶ πάντων ὧν τέτακταί σοι ποιῆσαι
verses.king_james: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum non viderem præ claritate luminis illius, ad manum deductus a comitibus, veni Damascum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ οὐκ ἐνέβλεπον ἀπὸ τῆς δόξης τοῦ φωτὸς ἐκείνου χειραγωγούμενος ὑπὸ τῶν συνόντων μοι ἦλθον εἰς δαμασκόν
verses.king_james: And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ananias autem quidam vir secundum legem, testimonium habens ab omnibus cohabitantibus Judæis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀνανίας δέ τις ἀνὴρ εὐσεβὴς κατὰ τὸν νόμον μαρτυρούμενος ὑπὸ πάντων τῶν κατοικούντων ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt *there*,

verses.latin_vulgate: veniens ad me et astans, dixit mihi: Saule frater, respice. Et ego eadem hora respexi in eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλθὼν πρός με καὶ ἐπιστὰς εἶπέν μοι σαοὺλ ἀδελφέ ἀνάβλεψον κἀγὼ αὐτῇ τῇ ὥρᾳ ἀνέβλεψα εἰς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Came unto me, and stood, and said unto me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And the same hour I looked up upon him.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille dixit: Deus patrum nostrorum præordinavit te, ut cognosceres voluntatem ejus, et videres justum, et audires vocem ex ore ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν ὁ θεὸς τῶν πατέρων ἡμῶν προεχειρίσατό σε γνῶναι τὸ θέλημα αὐτοῦ καὶ ἰδεῖν τὸν δίκαιον καὶ ἀκοῦσαι φωνὴν ἐκ τοῦ στόματος αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia eris testis illius ad omnes homines eorum quæ vidisti et audisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτι ἔσῃ μάρτυς αὐτῷ πρὸς πάντας ἀνθρώπους ὧν ἑώρακας καὶ ἤκουσας
verses.king_james: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc quid moraris? Exsurge, et baptizare, et ablue peccata tua, invocato nomine ipsius.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ νῦν τί μέλλεις ἀναστὰς βάπτισαι καὶ ἀπόλουσαι τὰς ἁμαρτίας σου ἐπικαλεσάμενος τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ κυρίου
verses.king_james: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Factum est autem revertenti mihi in Jerusalem, et oranti in templo, fieri me in stupore mentis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγένετο δέ μοι ὑποστρέψαντι εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ προσευχομένου μου ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ γενέσθαι με ἐν ἐκστάσει
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;

verses.latin_vulgate: et videre illum dicentem mihi: Festina, et exi velociter ex Jerusalem: quoniam non recipient testimonium tuum de me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδεῖν αὐτὸν λέγοντά μοι σπεῦσον καὶ ἔξελθε ἐν τάχει ἐξ ἰερουσαλήμ διότι οὐ παραδέξονταί σου τὴν μαρτυρίαν περὶ ἐμοῦ
verses.king_james: And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ego dixi: Domine, ipsi sciunt quia ego eram concludens in carcerem, et cædens per synagogas eos qui credebant in te:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀγὼ εἶπον κύριε αὐτοὶ ἐπίστανται ὅτι ἐγὼ ἤμην φυλακίζων καὶ δέρων κατὰ τὰς συναγωγὰς τοὺς πιστεύοντας ἐπὶ σέ
verses.king_james: And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: et cum funderetur sanguis Stephani testis tui, ego astabam, et consentiebam, et custodiebam vestimenta interficientium illum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτε ἐξεχεῖτο τὸ αἷμα στεφάνου τοῦ μάρτυρός σου καὶ αὐτὸς ἤμην ἐφεστὼς καὶ συνευδοκῶν τῇ ἀναιρέσει αὐτοῦ, καὶ φυλάσσων τὰ ἱμάτια τῶν ἀναιρούντων αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit ad me: Vade, quoniam ego in nationes longe mittam te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν πρός με πορεύου ὅτι ἐγὼ εἰς ἔθνη μακρὰν ἐξαποστελῶ σε
verses.king_james: And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audiebant autem eum usque ad hoc verbum, et levaverunt vocem suam, dicentes: Tolle de terra hujusmodi: non enim fas est eum vivere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤκουον δὲ αὐτοῦ ἄχρι τούτου τοῦ λόγου καὶ ἐπῆραν τὴν φωνὴν αὐτῶν λέγοντες αἶρε ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς τὸν τοιοῦτον οὐ γὰρ καθῆκον αὐτὸν ζῆν
verses.king_james: And they gave him audience unto this word, and *then* lifted up their voices, and said, Away with such a *fellow* from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vociferantibus autem eis, et projicientibus vestimenta sua, et pulverem jactantibus in aërem,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κραυγαζόντων δὲ αὐτῶν καὶ ῥιπτούντων τὰ ἱμάτια καὶ κονιορτὸν βαλλόντων εἰς τὸν ἀέρα
verses.king_james: And as they cried out, and cast off *their* clothes, and threw dust into the air,

verses.latin_vulgate: jussit tribunus induci eum in castra, et flagellis cædi, et torqueri eum, ut sciret propter quam causam sic acclamarent ei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκέλευσεν αὐτὸν ὁ χιλίαρχος ἄγεσθαι εἰς τὴν παρεμβολήν εἰπὼν μάστιξιν ἀνετάζεσθαι αὐτὸν ἵνα ἐπιγνῷ δι᾽ ἣν αἰτίαν οὕτως ἐπεφώνουν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum astrinxissent eum loris, dicit astanti sibi centurioni Paulus: Si hominem Romanum et indemnatum licet vobis flagellare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ προέτεινεν αὐτὸν τοῖς ἱμᾶσιν εἶπεν πρὸς τὸν ἑστῶτα ἑκατόνταρχον ὁ παῦλος εἰ ἄνθρωπον ῥωμαῖον καὶ ἀκατάκριτον ἔξεστιν ὑμῖν μαστίζειν
verses.king_james: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said unto the centurion that stood by, Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo audito, centurio accessit ad tribunum, et nuntiavit ei, dicens: Quid acturus es? hic enim homo civis Romanus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀκούσας δὲ ὁ ἑκατόνταρχος προσελθὼν ἀπήγγειλεν τῷ χιλιάρχῳ λέγων, ὅρα τί μέλλεις ποιεῖν ὁ γὰρ ἄνθρωπος οὗτος ῥωμαῖός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: When the centurion heard *that*, he went and told the chief captain, saying, Take heed what thou doest: for this man is a Roman.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accedens autem tribunus, dixit illi: Dic mihi si tu Romanus es? At ille dixit: Etiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσελθὼν δὲ ὁ χιλίαρχος εἶπεν αὐτῷ λέγε μοι εἶ σὺ ῥωμαῖος εἰ ὁ δὲ ἔφη ναί
verses.king_james: Then the chief captain came, and said unto him, Tell me, art thou a Roman? He said, Yea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondit tribunus: Ego multa summa civilitatem hanc consecutus sum. Et Paulus ait: Ego autem et natus sum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη τε ὁ χιλίαρχος ἐγὼ πολλοῦ κεφαλαίου τὴν πολιτείαν ταύτην ἐκτησάμην ὁ δὲ παῦλος ἔφη ἐγὼ δὲ καὶ γεγέννημαι
verses.king_james: And the chief captain answered, With a great sum obtained I this freedom. And Paul said, But I was *free* born.

verses.latin_vulgate: Protinus ergo discesserunt ab illo qui eum torturi erant. Tribunus quoque timuit postquam rescivit, quia civis Romanus esset, et quia alligasset eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐθέως οὖν ἀπέστησαν ἀπ᾽ αὐτοῦ οἱ μέλλοντες αὐτὸν ἀνετάζειν καὶ ὁ χιλίαρχος δὲ ἐφοβήθη ἐπιγνοὺς ὅτι ῥωμαῖός ἐστιν καὶ ὅτι ἦν αὐτὸν δεδεκώς
verses.king_james: Then straightway they departed from him which should have examined him: and the chief captain also was afraid, after he knew that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Postera autem die volens scire diligentius qua ex causa accusaretur a Judæis, solvit eum, et jussit sacerdotes convenire, et omne concilium: et producens Paulum, statuit inter illos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ δὲ ἐπαύριον βουλόμενος γνῶναι τὸ ἀσφαλὲς τὸ τί κατηγορεῖται παρὰ τῶν ἰουδαίων ἔλυσεν αὐτόν ἀπὸ τῶν δεσμῶν, καὶ ἐκέλευσεν ἐλθεῖν τοὺς ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ ὅλον τὸ συνέδριον αὐτῶν, καὶ καταγαγὼν τὸν παῦλον ἔστησεν εἰς αὐτούς
verses.king_james: On the morrow, because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him from *his* bands, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down, and set him before them.
